{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1105943929","pull_request_review_id":1297783990,"id":1105943929,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5B6115","diff_hunk":"@@ -1070,6 +1081,35 @@ function frexp(x::T) where T<:IEEEFloat
     return reinterpret(T, xu), k
 end
 
+"""
+    $(@__MODULE__).scaleinv(x)
+
+Compute `(scale, invscale)` where `scale` and `invscale` are non-subnormal
+(https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subnormal_number) finite powers of two such that
+`scale * invscale == 1` and `scale` is roughly on the order of `abs(x)`.","path":"base/math.jl","position":27,"original_position":27,"commit_id":"ecd234715db08fb2738bd31eb3aeaad97a01b39f","original_commit_id":"ecd234715db08fb2738bd31eb3aeaad97a01b39f","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"It's literally true that `eps(T) <= abs(x) * invscale < 4` except for when `x` is 0, Inf, or NaN (in which case the 0, Inf, or NaN is preserved by the finite `invscale`). However, for all `abs(x)` less than `inv(floatmin(T)) = prevfloat(floatmax(T)/4)` it holds that the product is `<2` and and for all `abs(x)` greater than `floatmin(T)` it holds that it's `>=1`.

I left the docstring vague to allow room to be flexible on the exact implementation and for future adjustments. #48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional #48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional speedup.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48645/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48645/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1436120356","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48645#issuecomment-1436120356","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48645","id":1436120356,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5VmXUk","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-02-19T22:55:18Z","updated_at":"2023-02-19T22:55:18Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"New timings with fastmath reinstated:

#48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional (re-do)","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Redoing #48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional      end
 end
 
+function _hypot(x::NTuple{N,T}) where {N,T<:IEEEFloat}
+    infT = convert(T, Inf)
+    x = abs.(x) # doesn't change result but enables computational shortcuts
+    # note: any() was causing this to not inline for N=3 but mapreduce() was not
+    mapreduce(==(infT), |, x) && return infT # return Inf even if an argument is NaN
+    maxabs = reinterpret(T, maximum(z -> reinterpret(Signed, z), x)) # for abs(::IEEEFloat) values, a ::BitInteger cast does not change the result
+    iszero(maxabs) && return maxabs
+    scale,invscale = scaleinv(maxabs)
+    return scale * sqrt(sum(y -> abs2(y * invscale), x))","path":"base/math.jl","position":null,"original_position":12,"commit_id":"c51c0f6a58d4b0a16844da0ffe9d7449911a56b4","original_commit_id":"ecd234715db08fb2738bd31eb3aeaad97a01b39f","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Done. #48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. ```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. #48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional speedup.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48645/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48645/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1436122884","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48645#issuecomment-1436122884","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48645","id":1436122884,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5VmX8E","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-02-19T23:02:35Z","updated_at":"2023-02-19T23:02:35Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I'd {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1111363091","pull_request_review_id":1304926155,"id":1111363091,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5CPg4T","diff_hunk":"@@ -845,6 +845,19 @@ function _hypot(x::NTuple{N,<:Number}) where {N}
     end
 end
 
+function _hypot(x::NTuple{N,T}) where {N,T<:IEEEFloat}
+    infT = convert(T, Inf)
+    x = abs.(x) # doesn't change result but enables computational shortcuts
+    # note: any() was causing this to not inline for N=3 but mapreduce() was not
+    mapreduce(==(infT), |, x) && return infT # return Inf even if an argument is NaN
+    maxabs = reinterpret(T, maximum(z -> reinterpret(Signed, z), x)) # for abs(::IEEEFloat) values, a ::BitInteger cast does not change the result
+    maxabs > zero(T) || return maxabs # catch NaN before the @fastmath below, but also shortcut 0 since we can (remove if no more @fastmath below)
+    scale,invscale = scaleinv(maxabs)
+     # @fastmath(+) to allow reassociation (see #48129)
+    add_fast(x, y) = Core.Intrinsics.add_float_fast(x, y) # @fastmath is not available during bootstrap

(re-do)","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Redoing #48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional (re-do)","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Redoing #48130 but without the `@fastmath` that was causing build problems. Performance is not quite as good as that PR but remains comparable for small numbers of inputs. The performance loss will be recovered if #48129 is resolved. In any case, it's a big improvement over master on an x86-64 architecture.

```julia-repl
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# this PR
  5.500 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.608 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.411 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.960 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The biggest difference is a replacing `maximum(abs,x)` with a specialized version for `IEEEFloats`. This performance difference is significant on x86-64 but may not exist on aarch64 since it has a native `max` instruction. This also uses fast scaling values (rather than division) to achieve additional thereof"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-02-21T09:22:28Z","updated_at":"2023-02-21T18:58:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Arithmetic operations on `Rational`s usually check for overflow, but `abs` doesn’t:
```julia
julia> x = typemin(Int) // 1
-9223372036854775808//1

julia> 2*x # detects overflow and errors
ERROR: OverflowError: 2 * -9223372036854775808 overflowed for type Int64
[...]

julia> abs(x) # doesn’t check for overflow, returns wrong result
-9223372036854775808//1

Since we check virtually all `Rational` arithmetic, I don't see further harm in checking `abs` as well.

```julia
# from rational.jl
abs(x::Rational) = Rational(abs(x.num), x.den)
```
Replacing the inner `abs` with `checked_abs` looks like it would be sufficient for this.

We should also check other places (at least within `rational.jl`) for dubious use of `abs`. On v1.8.0, the only other one I see is in `hash(x::Rational{<:BitInteger64}, h::UInt)`, where we check `abs(num) < 9007199254740992`. I think this would be fixed by checking `uabs(num) < 9007199254740992`, although I would advocate replacing `9007199254740992` by the equivalent but meaningful `UInt64(maxintfloat(Float64))`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1438956035/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48803","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48803/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48803/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48803/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48803","id":1600294761,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5KyOkE","number":48803,"title":"faster `mapreduce` for "reversed" views","user":{"login":"matthias314","id":56549971,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU2NTQ5OTcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/56549971?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314","html_url":"https://github.com/matthias314","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/matthias314/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2023-02-27T01:35:47Z","updated_at":"2023-02-27T16:34:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48803","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48803","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48803.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48803.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Since we now have fast iteration over `FastContiguousSubArray` (#48720), it could make sense to implement a special method for `mapreduce` for "reversed" `FastSubArray`, that is, for those step with value -1 (`w2` below). Since for `mapreduce` we are allowed to process the array elements in any order, we can reverse the view to get something that has a much better chance of SIMD compiler optimization. Here are some benchmarks:
```
g(a) = reduce(+, a; init = zero(eltype(a)))
v = rand(Int8, 2^16);
w1 = @view v[begin:end];
w2 = @view v[end:-1:begin];

julia> @btime g($v); @btime g($w1); @btime g($w2)

# master
  574.189 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  641.550 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  834.255 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
# this PR
  572.086 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)   # not affected by the PR
  675.769 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes)   # around 30ns slower
  696.621 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes)   # over 1000 times faster
```
The not implemented part of #48720 gave a speed-up factor of around 17 for `FastSubArray{Int8}`. Even if that is implemented later, this special method would still be around 70 times faster than that. Maybe this justifies to have a special method for this case. Maybe it doesn't.

**Note:** There is currently one issue with the PR. The new method allocates 48 bytes, which seems to take around 30ns (the time difference for `w1` above). I don't know how to get rid of this, but I'm hoping that somebody else has an idea. I have the feeling that it is somehow related to the treatment of keyword arguments.

Also note that I'm using `invoke` to avoid any interference with the implementation of `mapreduce` for `AbstractArray`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48803/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48803/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1446655579","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48803#issuecomment-1446655579","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48803","id":1446655579,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5WOjZb","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-02-27T16:34:30Z","updated_at":"2023-02-27T16:34:30Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> Or restrict to known commutative operations.

I really dislike this. It has no support or extensibility for new operations. It's not just single primitive operations on primitive types that can SIMD, it's also compositions of those operations. Likewise, it can support other types that wrap these primitive types for slightly different behavior (which may or may not change the behavior on the suggested operations). It's also unnecessarily fragile to evil users doing evil piracy, even if they've likely doomed themselves in other ways.

I'd much prefer we use a `Base.FlipArgs(op)` composition and let the compiler do its thing. If "the compiler doing its thing" is dumb, that's an orthogonal issue.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1446655579/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

# current: with ===

```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48109/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48109/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1370432266","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48109#issuecomment-1370432266","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48109","id":1370432266,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5RryMK","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-01-04T02:54:11Z","updated_at":"2023-01-04T02:54:11Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"The relevant x86-64 assembly from `code_native` on `Float64` input:
```
## original
        vsubsd  %xmm0, %xmm0, %xmm1
        vxorpd  %xmm2, %xmm2, %xmm2
        vcmpordpd       %xmm2, %xmm0, %xmm0
        vcmpneqpd       %xmm2, %xmm1, %xmm1
        vandpd  %xmm1, %xmm0, %xmm0
        vmovd   %xmm0, %eax
        andb    $1, %al
        retq

## == version
        movabsq $.LCPI0_0, %rax
        vandpd  (%rax), %xmm0, %xmm0
        movabsq $.LCPI0_1, %rax
        vucomisd        (%rax), %xmm0
        setae   %al
        retq

## === version
        movb    $63, %cl
        bzhiq   %rcx, %rax, %rax
        movabsq $9218868437227405312, %rcx      # imm = 0x7FF0000000000000
        cmpq    %rcx, %rax
        sete    %al
        retq
```

The original version just had 2 conditions to check (and didn't find a way to do it better) while the PR version only checks 1. The PR version only requires zeroing the top bit of the input and comparing to the literal `Inf`. All the instructions are pretty basic (I think), so I'd just go with the instruction count: 7 for the original and 5 for either the `==` or `===` versions here.

The inner loop of `any(isinf,::Vector{Float64})`:
```
## original
.LBB0_5:                                # %L31
                                        # =>This Inner Loop Header: Depth=1
        cmpq    %rsi, %rcx
        je      .LBB0_6
# %bb.7:                                # %L43
                                        #   in Loop: Header=BB0_5 Depth=1
        vmovsd  (%rdx,%rsi,8), %xmm1            # xmm1 = mem[0],zero
        incq    %rsi
        vucomisd        %xmm1, %xmm1
        jp      .LBB0_5
# %bb.8:                                # %L43
                                        #   in Loop: Header=BB0_5 Depth=1
        vsubsd  %xmm1, %xmm1, %xmm1
        vucomisd        %xmm0, %xmm1
        jne     .LBB0_9
        jnp     .LBB0_5

## === version
.LBB0_4:                                # %L26
                                        # =>This Inner Loop Header: Depth=1
        cmpq    %rdx, %rcx
        je      .LBB0_5
# %bb.6:                                # %L38
                                        #   in Loop: Header=BB0_4 Depth=1
        bzhiq   %r9, (%rsi,%rdx,8), %rdi
        incq    %rdx
        cmpq    %r8, %rdi
        jne     .LBB0_4
```

The `any` versions show the benefits more clearly, hoisting the `mov` instructions and leaving 10 instructions for the base version and 6 in the PR.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1370432266/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48129","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48129/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48129/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48129/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48129","id":1520049195,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5amhwr","number":48129,"title":"`reduce(op, ::NTuple)` does not re-associate operations","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-01-05T04:45:37Z","updated_at":"2023-01-05T04:45:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`reduce` misses the opportunity to re-associate operations when given `NTuple` arguments. This has performance impacts on notable functions such as `sum(::NTuple)`. One can use `@fastmath` to permit re-association in some cases, but I'm never a fan of suggesting `@fastmath` to people.

```julia-repl
julia> using BenchmarkTools

julia> x = ntuple(_->randn(), 16);

julia> @btime sum($x)
  5.700 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
-2.8324452894507908

julia> @btime @fastmath(reduce(+,$x))
  3.100 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
-2.8324452894507903
```
You can see the lack of vectorization by comparing
`code_native(sum,Tuple{NTuple{16,Float64}};debuginfo=:none)`
to the SIMDing version
`code_native(x->@fastmath(reduce(+,x)),Tuple{NTuple{16,Float64}};debuginfo=:none)`.

Methods like `sum(::Vector)` already do re-associate (with performance and accuracy benefits).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48129/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48129/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":48130,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48130","id":1185892073,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5Gr0bp","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48130","number":48130,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"faster `hypot(::IEEEFloat...)`","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"`hypot(::IEEEFloat...)` can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The notable savings here come from casting to integers before computing `maximum(abs,...)` (same result but faster comparisons) and re-associating the final sum (see      end
 end
 
+function _hypot(x::NTuple{N,T}) where {N,T<:IEEEFloat}
+    infT = convert(T, Inf)
+    x = abs.(x) # doesn't change result but enables computational shortcuts
+    any(==(infT), x) && return infT # return Inf even if an argument is NaN","path":"base/math.jl","position":16,"original_position":16,"commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","original_commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"I'd tried this but the performance difference isn't as negligible as I'd like on longer tuples:
```julia-repl
# use `isinf`
  7.708 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  7.908 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.111 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.860 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
Looking at the `code_native`, the `isinf` version turns into a branch chain (with short-circuit evaluation) while `==(Inf)` (which is valid only because we already `abs`'d the values) results in branchless SIMD.

So there are three options:
1) use `==(Inf)` here
2) use `isinf` here but do not change it (performance penalty)
3) use `isinf` here after changing it to use `==` instead of `===` (I verified this gives the same assembly as the current version)

Since that `isinf` change is less than a day old and the performance difference between `==` and `===` was very small, I'd vote for option 3.","created_at":"2023-01-05T05:29:30Z","updated_at":"2023-01-05T05:29:30Z","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130#discussion_r1062120954","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48130","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1062120954"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130#discussion_r1062120954"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48130"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1062120954/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":847,"original_line":847,"side":"RIGHT","in_reply_to_id":1062112634},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48130","id":1185892073,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5Gr0bp","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48130","number":48130,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"faster can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The notable savings here come from casting to integers before computing `maximum(abs,...)` (same result but faster comparisons) and re-associating the final sum (see      end
 end
 
+function _hypot(x::NTuple{N,T}) where {N,T<:IEEEFloat}
+    infT = convert(T, Inf)
+    x = abs.(x) # doesn't change result but enables computational shortcuts
+    any(==(infT), x) && return infT # return Inf even if an argument is NaN","path":"base/math.jl","position":16,"original_position":16,"commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","original_commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"The issue is that LLVM fails to recognize that `x===c` and `x==c` are equivalent for any value `c` except `-0.0`, `+0.0`, or `NaN`. Thus, it always does `x===c` using integer instructions and `x==c` using float instructions. The relevant x64-64 assembly to the `any(isinf,x)` check:
```
#### with ===
        vmovq   %xmm1, %rcx
        movabsq $.LCPI0_1, %rdx                                                            # +Inf
        vmovsd  (%rdx), %xmm0                   # xmm0 = mem[0],zero       # place +Inf as the possible return value (not relevant to this code section)
        cmpq    %rax, %rcx
        je      .LBB0_6
# %bb.1:                                # %L18.lr.ph
        vpextrq $1, %xmm1, %rdx
        cmpq    %rax, %rdx
        je      .LBB0_6
# %bb.2:                                # %L18.1
        vextracti128    $1, %ymm1, %xmm2
        vmovq   %xmm2, %rsi
        cmpq    %rax, %rsi
        je      .LBB0_6
# %bb.3:                                # %L18.2
        vpextrq $1, %xmm2, %rdi
        cmpq    %rax, %rdi
        je      .LBB0_6

#### with ==
        movabsq $.LCPI0_1, %rax                                                         # +Inf
        vbroadcastsd    (%rax), %ymm0
        vcmpeqpd        %ymm0, %ymm1, %ymm0
        vmovmskpd       %ymm0, %ecx
        testl   %ecx, %ecx
        je      .LBB0_3
```","created_at":"2023-01-05T15:16:01Z","updated_at":"2023-01-05T15:16:01Z","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130#discussion_r1062587082","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48130","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1062587082"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130#discussion_r1062587082"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48130"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1062587082/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":847,"original_line":847,"side":"RIGHT","in_reply_to_id":1062112634},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48130","id":1185892073,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5Gr0bp","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48130","number":48130,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"faster `hypot(::IEEEFloat...)`","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"`hypot(::IEEEFloat...)` can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The notable savings here come from casting to integers before computing `maximum(abs,...)` (same result but faster comparisons) and re-associating the final sum (see `hypot(::IEEEFloat...)`","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"`hypot(::IEEEFloat...)` can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The notable savings here come from casting to integers before computing `maximum(abs,...)` (same result but faster comparisons) and re-associating the final sum (see can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

minimum/maximum/extrema","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR uses a new method to greatly accelerate `minimum`/`maximum`/`extrema` for `IEEEFloat` values. The concept is to convert floats to signed integers that respect the same ordering that `min`/`max` do for floats. Since integers can be min/max'd much more quickly than floats, this leads to a considerable speedup. This is a minor extension to the concept used by `isless` since #39090. I used the changes here to slightly accelerate that version of `isless` as well.

Note that the old versions had special-cased `_mapreduce_impl` for `min`/`max`. The reckless application of these led to very poor performance for integer `minimum`/`maximum`. This implementation eliminates those specializations, improving integer performance as well.

Benchmark:
```
using BenchmarkTools
a = randn(1000);
b = zeros(1000);
c = @. ifelse(rand() < 0.1, missing, a);
j = rand(Int,1000);
@btime minimum($a); # "typical" case
@btime minimum($b); # pathological case for old version
@btime extrema($a);
@btime minimum($c); # with missing
@btime minimum($j); # integer performance
@btime searchsortedlast($a, x) setup=(x=randn()); # isless performance
```

Before:
```
  812.222 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  1.620 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  4.629 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  1.220 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  262.315 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.729 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
This PR:
```
  173.764 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  173.721 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  278.276 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  954.839 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  92.453 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.311 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
~#43725:~ EDIT: never mind - it sounds like I didn't get that benchmark right

Results are similar for `maximum`.
This idea does not support values that may be `Missing` whereas the original specialization did. Interestingly, eliminating that specialization (none of the new code here is run with `Missing`) still led to a performance improvement.

For `Float64`, this PR makes `minimum`/`maximum` 4.7x faster (more in pathological case), `extrema` 16x faster, and `isless` 1.3x faster. Note that `sort` already has specializations for floats, which remains faster than the naive application of this updated `isless`. This also makes `minimum`/`maximum` 2.8x faster for `Int`.


The technical idea here is finished, but I could use some help on the architecture. The current version makes `_mapreduce(f,op,...)` check for whether a registered acceleration function exists for `op` for the type of `f` applied to the return type. It only takes effect if the `return_type` of `f` applied to the input is known to be a `IEEEFloat`. In the case that such a function exists, it wraps `f` in the accelerating transformation and applies an appropriate inverse transformation to the result. minimum/maximum/extrema","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR uses a new method to greatly accelerate `minimum`/`maximum`/`extrema` for `IEEEFloat` values. The concept is to convert floats to signed integers that respect the same ordering that `min`/`max` do for floats. Since integers can be min/max'd much more quickly than floats, this leads to a considerable speedup. This is a minor extension to the concept used by `isless` since #39090. I used the changes here to slightly accelerate that version of `isless` as well.

Note that the old versions had special-cased `_mapreduce_impl` for `min`/`max`. The reckless application of these led to very poor performance for integer `minimum`/`maximum`. This implementation eliminates those specializations, improving integer performance as well.

Benchmark:
```
using BenchmarkTools
a = randn(1000);
b = zeros(1000);
c = @. ifelse(rand() < 0.1, missing, a);
j = rand(Int,1000);
@btime minimum($a); # "typical" case
@btime minimum($b); # pathological case for old version
@btime extrema($a);
@btime minimum($c); # with missing
@btime minimum($j); # integer performance
@btime searchsortedlast($a, x) setup=(x=randn()); # isless performance
```

Before:
```
  812.222 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  1.620 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  4.629 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  1.220 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  262.315 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.729 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
This PR:
```
  173.764 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  173.721 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  278.276 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  954.839 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  92.453 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  11.311 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
~#43725:~ EDIT: never mind - it sounds like I didn't get that benchmark right

Results are similar for `maximum`.
This idea does not support values that may be `Missing` whereas the original specialization did. Interestingly, eliminating that specialization (none of the new code here is run with `Missing`) still led to a performance improvement.

For `Float64`, this PR makes `minimum`/`maximum` 4.7x faster (more in pathological case), `extrema` 16x faster, and `isless` 1.3x faster. Note that `sort` already has specializations for floats, which remains faster than the naive application of this updated `isless`. This also makes `minimum`/`maximum` 2.8x faster for `Int`.


The technical idea here is finished, but I could use some help on the architecture. The current version makes `_mapreduce(f,op,...)` check for whether a registered acceleration function exists for `op` for the type of `f` applied to the return type. It only takes effect if the `return_type` of `f` applied to the input is known to be a `IEEEFloat`. In the case that such a function exists, it wraps `f` in the accelerating transformation and applies an appropriate inverse transformation to the result. `hypot(::IEEEFloat...)`","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"`hypot(::IEEEFloat...)` can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The notable savings here come from casting to integers before computing `maximum(abs,...)` (same result but faster comparisons) and re-associating the final sum (see      end
 end
 
+function _hypot(x::NTuple{N,T}) where {N,T<:IEEEFloat}
+    infT = convert(T, Inf)
+    x = abs.(x) # doesn't change result but enables computational shortcuts
+    any(==(infT), x) && return infT # return Inf even if an argument is NaN","path":"base/math.jl","position":16,"original_position":16,"commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","original_commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"The branching with `===` disappears if I use `mapreduce(isinf,|,x)` instead of `any(isinf,x)`. However, it still doesn't SIMD and the performance difference doesn't change noticeably (`Inf` never appears in my benchmark evaluation, so the branches should be predicted very well). I don't know why LLVM is letting us down here, except that maybe the integer SIMD instructions don't exist or it isn't as smart about using them.

Any thoughts on the 3 options I listed above? In #48109, we definitely saw that the `===` version of `isinf` was very slightly faster than `==`. To make sure our original benchmarks of `isinf` weren't overly trivial, I benchmarked where `any(isinf,...)` was used for branch selection and it also slightly favored `===`. But here we do see a practical case where `==` results in a notable performance benefit. Honestly, all my intuition would have predicted (incorrectly) that `===` would be at least as good as `==` here since the values are used as integers in the `maximum` that immediately follows.

I can't predict how this would play out across a larger set of practical usages, except that the few other toy benchmarks I've tried tie or favor `===`. My only argument for `==` (beyond the specific case arising in this PR) is that it may be marginally easier for LLVM to optimize if it can someday figure out that it can alternatively use `===` for many comparisons (figuring out the reverse direction seems tougher, or at least not easier).

The evidenced option (and the coward's option) is to keep `isinf` using `===` and this function using `==` even if the discrepancy seems can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The notable savings here come from casting to integers before computing `maximum(abs,...)` (same result but faster comparisons) and re-associating the final sum (see can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

The notable savings here come from casting to integers before computing `maximum(abs,...)` (same result but faster comparisons) and re-associating the final sum (see #48129).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48130/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48130/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1374611962","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48130#issuecomment-1374611962","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48130","id":1374611962,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5R7un6","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-01-07T21:04:22Z","updated_at":"2023-01-07T21:04:22Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"In any case, the issue was fixed before any efforts from this PR (or otherwise never existed on my architecture).  function _hypot(x::NTuple{N,<:Number}) where {N}
     maxabs = maximum(abs, x)
-    if isnan(maxabs) && any(isinf, x)
+    if any(isinf, x)","path":"base/math.jl","position":5,"original_position":5,"commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","original_commit_id":"1a0340caf7243d7e0bf76fad1de30e046a3e4e59","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"IEEE754 defines `hypot(Inf,NaN) == Inf`. Try interpreting `NaN` as "an unknown real number or infinity" and you'll see that the semantics make sense.

This change doesn't do what you think it does. Take a careful look and you'll see that the `isnan` check only served to skip the `any(isinf,...)` check when `maxabs` isn't `NaN`, because the following line will catch it anyway. But what I have messed up here is that with this change, the `isinf(maxabs)` check that immediately follows is redundant.

But on second thought, I'll revert this change anyway. I don't think that particular method will get called very often since the new `IEEEFloat` version should very often shadow it. Perhaps with whatever exotic types do make it to this method (note that `BigFloat` also has its own method) the short-circuiting may be can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

can be made faster for 3+ arguments.

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

t3 = ntuple(_->randn(), 3)
t4 = ntuple(_->randn(), 4)
t8 = ntuple(_->randn(), 8)
t16 = ntuple(_->randn(), 16)

@btime hypot($t3...)
@btime hypot($t4...)
@btime hypot($t8...)
@btime hypot($t16...)

# master
  9.810 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.110 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  19.759 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  50.203 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

# SHA 1a0340c
  7.808 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.008 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  10.010 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  14.529 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

release-1.9"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":31,"created_at":"2023-01-06T21:06:28Z","updated_at":"2023-01-09T16:40:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The following script works on Julia 1.8.4

```julia
using DelimitedFiles
temp = tempname()
open(temp, "w") do io
    println(io, "1,2,3")
end
println(sum(readdlm(temp, ',', Int)))
```

Ideally it should work on all future Julia versions. In interactive use, once `DelimitedFiles.jl` is no longer a stdlib, the user will be prompted to install the package at the REPL. In package use, it should already be in the environment. As a scripts without a Project.toml, IIUC, the plan is to make this fail with `ERROR: ArgumentError: Package DelimitedFiles not found in current path.`.

An alternate proposal from @IanButterworth on slack is

> for scripts, if the package name cannot be resolved, and its in a special list known to Julia of names that used to be stdlibs, then auto-install it and carry on

Much like the interactive REPL prompt, but install & warn rather than prompt.

EDIT to clarify: This would be backed by downloading and loading DelimitedFiles with Pkg, not by packaging DelimitedFiles with Julia.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48161/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":2,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48161/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1375926084","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48161#issuecomment-1375926084","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48161","id":1375926084,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5SAvdE","user":{"login":"mikmoore","id":95002244,"node_id":"U_kgDOBamehA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95002244?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore","html_url":"https://github.com/mikmoore","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mikmoore/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-01-09T16:40:06Z","updated_at":"2023-01-09T16:40:06Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> A more accurate error message for this case, saying that the stdlib has been moved and to do `Pkg.add("DelimitedFiles")` is enough imho.

I wouldn't bother to explain that the package has been moved. I think that's noise necessary. An experienced user will shrug and know exactly how to fix it (even if they're surprised by the error) while a "pure consumer" won't care. The suggestion to install it (and copy-paste code) is sufficient and generalized to non-stdlib packages as well.

The error message is great for real packages (especially with the interactive prompt)
```julia-repl
julia> using Tullio
 │ Package Tullio not found, but a package named Tullio is available from a registry.
 │ Install package?
 │   (@v1.8) pkg> add Tullio
 └ (y/n/o) [y]: n
ERROR: ArgumentError: Package Tullio not found in current path.
- Run `import Pkg; Pkg.add("Tullio")` to install the Tullio package.
```
but is misleading for packages that aren't available from a known registry
```julia-repl
julia> using NotARealPackage
ERROR: ArgumentError: Package NotARealPackage not found in current path.
- Run `import Pkg; Pkg.add("NotARealPackage")` to install the NotARealPackage package.
```
I would suggest that we give a different error message when the package is not known. On the other hand, they'll get to the error if they try the suggestion and maybe deferring this this means we don't have to go check the package server right now (which may have some merit).

It may also be worth considering that if we're running from the command line (ie `isinteractive()==false`) that the command be given not as `import Pkg; Pkg.add(...)` but as something copy-pastable to a command line (a la `julia -e "import Pkg; Pkg.add(...)"`) to be more helpful to script callers who may be unfamiliar with Julia.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1375926084/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

#38274

In particular:

```julia-repl
julia> function f(obj)
         obj::String
         length(obj)
       end
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> code_typed(f, (Any,), optimize=false)
1-element Vector{Any}:
 CodeInfo(
1 ─      (obj = Core.typeassert(obj, Main.String))::String
│   %2 = Main.length(obj::String)::Int64
└──      return %2
) => Int64
```

before

```julia-repl
julia> code_typed(f, (Any,), optimize=false)
1-element Vector{Any}:
 CodeInfo(
1 ─      Core.typeassert(obj, Main.String)::String
│   %2 = Main.length(obj)::Any
└──      return %2
) => Any
```

#38274

In particular:

```julia-repl
julia> function f(obj)
         obj::String
         length(obj)
       end
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> code_typed(f, (Any,), optimize=false)
1-element Vector{Any}:
 CodeInfo(
1 ─      (obj = Core.typeassert(obj, Main.String))::String
│   %2 = Main.length(obj::String)::Int64
└──      return %2
) => Int64
```

before

```julia-repl
julia> code_typed(f, (Any,), optimize=false)
1-element Vector{Any}:
 CodeInfo(
1 ─      Core.typeassert(obj, Main.String)::String
│   %2 = Main.length(obj)::Any
└──      return %2
) => Any
```

```julia-repl
julia> function lassert(); x::Int = 1; x = "just kidding"; end
lassert (generic function with 1 method)

julia> function rassert(); x = 1::Int; x = "just kidding"; end
rassert (generic function with 1 method)

julia> lassert()
ERROR: MethodError: Cannot `convert` an object of type String to an object of type Int64
Closest candidates are:
  convert(::Type{T}, ::Ptr) where T<:Integer at pointer.jl:23
  convert(::Type{T}, ::T) where T<:Number at number.jl:6
  convert(::Type{T}, ::Number) where T<:Number at number.jl:7
  ...
Stacktrace:
 [1] lassert()
   @ Main ./REPL[483]:1
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[485]:1

julia> rassert()
"just kidding"
```
But maybe this is handled in some way I don't understand. I don't understand the lowering process itself.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1379285358/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
